WARNING: This data is ONLY for CSE and CS branch.
course_objectives: To develop a strong foundation in database management concepts.
To equip students with practical skills in database design, normalization, transaction management, and recovery techniques.
course_outcomes: CO1: Acquire knowledge of database design methodology for real-life applications
CO2: Design an information model using the concept of ER diagram
CO3: Apply the concept of SQL on real-life databases
CO4: Analyze the redundancy problem in the database and reduce it using normalization.
CO5: Identify the broad range of database management issues including data integrity, security, and recovery transactions.

units.title: Introduction to Database System
topics: Entity Relationship Model: ER Model Concepts
Reduction of ER Diagrams to Tables
Use Case: Banking System, National Hockey League (NHL)

units.title: Relational Data Model
topics: Integrity Constraints: Entity integrity and referential integrity
Relational Algebra: SELECT, PROJECT, CROSS, JOIN, DIVISION
Basic Queries: CREATE, SELECT, INSERT, DELETE, UPDATE
Sub Queries, Aggregate Functions, Joins, Unions, Intersection, Minus, Views

units.title: Transaction Processing
topics: Time Stamping Protocols for Concurrency Control

units.title: Database Recovery Techniques
topics: DDL within PL/SQL, DML in PL/SQL

textbooks: Elmasri, Navathe - Fundamentals of Database Systems, Addison Wesley
Korth, Silbertz, Sudarshan - Database Concepts, McGraw Hill
Date C J - An Introduction to Database Systems, Addison Wesley
reference_books: Bipin C. Desai - An Introduction to Database Systems, Gagotia Publications
RAMAKRISHNAN - Database Management Systems, McGraw Hill
